claim: Agentic engineering workflow requires GPT-5.5 Extra High Fast in Cursor paired with Greptile code review and WhisperFlow speech-to-text
actionable: Use GPT-5.5 Extra High Fast as your primary model in Cursor, integrate Greptile for multi-turn code review feedback loops, and WhisperFlow for voice prompting
source_title: My Agentic Engineering Workflow (step by step workflow)

claim: Grep Loop automates iterative code review by reading Greptile feedback, fixing issues, pushing changes, and re-reviewing until 5/5 confidence
actionable: Install Greptile's grep-loop skill in Cursor to automate the fix-review-push cycle, which will continue until your code review score is 5/5 or reaches 5-6 iterations
source_title: My Agentic Engineering Workflow (step by step workflow)

claim: WhisperFlow's free tier is so generous that many power users haven't needed to pay for it despite heavy use over months
actionable: Use WhisperFlow's free tier for speech-to-text prompting rather than typing; it accelerates prompt generation and maintains conversational flow
source_title: My Agentic Engineering Workflow (step by step workflow)

claim: Claude artifacts should be previewable in a resizable side panel to match desktop app UI patterns
actionable: When building artifact-preview features, implement resizable panels that match native desktop app patterns so users can adjust preview size on the fly
source_title: My Agentic Engineering Workflow (step by step workflow)

claim: Agent-generated code can ship with streaming HTML visible if the user experience is poor; disable streaming and show 'crafting artifact' instead
actionable: When agents generate HTML, replace live HTML streaming with an animated 'crafting artifact' message, then show the complete result once done
source_title: My Agentic Engineering Workflow (step by step workflow)

claim: Artifact version history should always be visible and user-accessible so every update can be traced and reverted
actionable: Maintain complete artifact version history in your UI, showing V1, V2, etc., and allow users to view or revert to any previous version
source_title: My Agentic Engineering Workflow (step by step workflow)

claim: Multi-step artifacts (like combined World War I and II history) require stable artifact keys so the agent can append to existing artifacts
actionable: Implement stable artifact keys so agents know when they are updating an existing artifact versus creating a new one
source_title: My Agentic Engineering Workflow (step by step workflow)

claim: Stacked PRs (dependent PRs on branches stacked on each other) enable safer rollout of large features through incremental code review
actionable: When implementing complex features with agents, split into 3-4 stacked PRs (PR-87 -> PR-88 -> PR-89) so each PR under 1000 lines can be reviewed independently
source_title: My Agentic Engineering Workflow (step by step workflow)

claim: Even with 3+ iterations of Greptile review, large PRs (2000+ lines) may still have unfixed edge cases because the agent can't hold all context
actionable: If your PR is over 2000 lines and you get stuck at 3/5 code review score, split into smaller PRs rather than hoping more iterations will fix everything
source_title: My Agentic Engineering Workflow (step by step workflow)

claim: Plans themselves are primarily written for the human developer's memory, not strictly for the agent's execution
actionable: When managing multiple concurrent features, write explicit plans as much for your own recall of context as for the agent's execution
source_title: My Agentic Engineering Workflow (step by step workflow)

claim: Using SvelteKit, Convex, Electron, and Daytona creates a composable architecture that agents can reliably understand and modify
actionable: Choose tech stacks with clear service boundaries (e.g., Svelte for UI, Convex for backend, distinct admin dashboard) so agents can isolate changes
source_title: My Agentic Engineering Workflow (step by step workflow)
